content.text: I found the reported order: `process.rs` drops privileges before `sandbox::apply`, and `landlock.rs` opens paths inside `apply`, so the next step is to inspect the Landlock API and nearby call sites to design a safe two-phase fix.

I found the core ordering issue in both process spawning paths and the SSH pre-exec path: privileges are dropped before Landlock opens rule paths and applies restrictions. I’ll track the fix steps and then add a temporary regression test before implementing.

[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
version = "0.0.0"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.88"
license = "Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell"

[workspace.dependencies]
# Async runtime
tokio = { version = "1.43", features = ["full"] }

# gRPC/Protobuf
tonic = "0.12"
tonic-build = "0.12"
prost = "0.13"
prost-types = "0.13"

# HTTP server
axum = { version = "0.8", features = ["ws"] }
tower = "0.5"
tower-http = { version = "0.6", features = ["cors", "trace"] }
hyper = { version = "1.6", features = ["full"] }
hyper-util = { version = "0.1", features = ["tokio", "server-auto"] }
http = "1.2"
http-body = "1.0"
http-body-util = "0.1"

# TLS
tokio-rustls = { version = "0.26", default-features = false, features = ["logging", "tls12", "ring"] }
rustls = { version = "0.23", default-features = false, features = ["std", "logging", "tls12", "ring"] }
rustls-pemfile = "2"
rcgen = { version = "0.13", features = ["crypto", "pem"] }
webpki-roots = "1"

# CLI
clap = { version = "4.5", features = ["derive", "env"] }
clap_complete = { version = "4.5", features = ["unstable-dynamic"] }
indicatif = "0.17"
owo-colors = "4"
ratatui = "0.26"
crossterm = "0.28"
terminal-colorsaurus = "1.0"

# Error handling
miette = { version = "7", features = ["fancy"] }
thiserror = "2"
anyhow = "1"

# Logging/Tracing
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "json"] }
tracing-appender = "0.2"

# Unix/Process
nix = { version = "0.29", features = ["signal", "process", "user", "fs", "term"] }

# Serialization
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
serde_yml = "0.0.12"

# HTTP client
reqwest = { version = "0.12", default-features = false, features = ["json", "rustls-tls"] }

# WebSocket
tokio-tungstenite = { version = "0.26", features = ["rustls-tls-native-roots"] }

# Clipboard (OSC 52)
base64 = "0.22"

# Filesystem embedding
include_dir = "0.7"

# Glob matching
glob = "0.3"

# Utilities
futures = "0.3"
bytes = "1"
pin-project-lite = "0.2"
tokio-stream = "0.1"
protobuf-src = "1.1.0"
url = "2"

# Database
sqlx = { version = "0.8", features = ["runtime-tokio-rustls", "postgres", "sqlite", "migrate"] }

# Kubernetes
kube = { version = "0.90", features = ["runtime", "derive"] }
kube-runtime = "0.90"
k8s-openapi = { version = "0.21.1", features = ["v1_26"] }

# IDs
uuid = { version = "1.10", features = ["v4"] }

# SMT solver (uses system libz3; enable z3/bundled via the prover's bundled-z3 feature for local dev without system z3)
z3 = "0.19"

[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "warn"
rust_2018_idioms = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }
trivial_casts = "warn"
trivial_numeric_casts = "warn"
unused_lifetimes = "warn"
unused_qualifications = "warn"

[workspace.lints.clippy]
all = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }
pedantic = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }
nursery = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }

# Allow certain pedantic lints that are too noisy
module_name_repetitions = "allow"
must_use_candidate = "allow"
missing_errors_doc = "allow"
missing_panics_doc = "allow"

# Allow noisy nursery lints
significant_drop_tightening = "allow"  # Often gives incorrect suggestions
missing_const_for_fn = "allow"  # Too noisy for async code patterns

# Allow noisy pedantic lints
too_many_lines = "allow"  # Function length limits are subjective
needless_pass_by_value = "allow"  # Common pattern in async handlers
ref_option = "allow"  # Common pattern for optional references
missing_fields_in_debug = "allow"  # Manual Debug impls often intentionally omit fields

[profile.release]
strip = true

[profile.dev]
# Faster compile times for dev builds
debug = 1

+        // Landlock path FDs and the ruleset are prepared by the root
+        // supervisor before fork. Only restrict_self runs in the child.
+        // Drop privileges after Landlock is active. initgroups/setgid/setuid
+        // need access to /etc/group and /etc/passwd, so Landlock preparation
+        // happens earlier while the supervisor is still unrestricted.

3	
4	//! Process management and signal handling.
5	
6	use crate::child_env;
7	use crate::policy::{NetworkMode, SandboxPolicy};
8	use crate::sandbox;
9	#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
10	use crate::sandbox::linux::netns::NetworkNamespace;
11	#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
12	use crate::{register_managed_child, unregister_managed_child};
13	use miette::{IntoDiagnostic, Result};
14	use nix::sys::signal::{self, Signal};
15	use nix::unistd::{Group, Pid, User};
16	use std::collections::HashMap;
17	use std::ffi::CString;
18	#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
19	use std::os::unix::io::RawFd;
20	use std::path::PathBuf;
21	use std::process::Stdio;
22	use tokio::process::{Child, Command};
23	use tracing::debug;
24	
25	const SSH_HANDSHAKE_SECRET_ENV: &str = "OPENSHELL_SSH_HANDSHAKE_SECRET";
26	
27	fn inject_provider_env(cmd: &mut Command, provider_env: &HashMap<String, String>) {
28	    for (key, value) in provider_env {
29	        cmd.env(key, value);
30	    }
31	}
32	
33	fn scrub_sensitive_env(cmd: &mut Command) {
34	    cmd.env_remove(SSH_HANDSHAKE_SECRET_ENV);
35	}
36	
37	/// Handle to a running process.
38	pub struct ProcessHandle {
39	    child: Child,
40	    pid: u32,
41	}
42	
43	impl ProcessHandle {
44	    /// Spawn a new process.
45	    ///
46	    /// # Errors
47	    ///
48	    /// Returns an error if the process fails to start.
49	    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
50	    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
51	    pub fn spawn(
52	        program: &str,
53	        args: &[String],
54	        workdir: Option<&str>,
55	        interactive: bool,
56	        policy: &SandboxPolicy,
57	        netns: Option<&NetworkNamespace>,
58	        ca_paths: Option<&(PathBuf, PathBuf)>,
59	        provider_env: &HashMap<String, String>,
60	    ) -> Result<Self> {
61	        Self::spawn_impl(
62	            program,
63	            args,
64	            workdir,
65	            interactive,
66	            policy,
67	            netns.and_then(NetworkNamespace::ns_fd),
68	            ca_paths,
69	            provider_env,
70	        )
71	    }
72	
73	    /// Spawn a new process (non-Linux platforms).
74	    ///
75	    /// # Errors
76	    ///
77	    /// Returns an error if the process fails to start.
78	    #[cfg(not(target_os = "linux"))]
79	    pub fn spawn(
80	        program: &str,
81	        args: &[String],
82	        workdir: Option<&str>,
83	        interactive: bool,
84	        policy: &SandboxPolicy,
85	        ca_paths: Option<&(PathBuf, PathBuf)>,
86	        provider_env: &HashMap<String, String>,
87	    ) -> Result<Self> {
88	        Self::spawn_impl(
89	            program,
90	            args,
91	            workdir,
92	            interactive,
93	            policy,
94	            ca_paths,
95	            provider_env,
96	        )
97	    }
98	
99	    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
100	    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
101	    fn spawn_impl(
102	        program: &str,
103	        args: &[String],
104	        workdir: Option<&str>,
105	        interactive: bool,
106	        policy: &SandboxPolicy,
107	        netns_fd: Option<RawFd>,
108	        ca_paths: Option<&(PathBuf, PathBuf)>,
109	        provider_env: &HashMap<String, String>,
110	    ) -> Result<Self> {
111	        let mut cmd = Command::new(program);
112	        cmd.args(args)
113	            .stdin(Stdio::inherit())
114	            .stdout(Stdio::inherit())
115	            .stderr(Stdio::inherit())
116	            .kill_on_drop(true)
117	            .env("OPENSHELL_SANDBOX", "1");
118	
119	        scrub_sensitive_env(&mut cmd);
120	        inject_provider_env(&mut cmd, provider_env);

content.text:
Implemented the Landlock enforcement fix without committing changes.

Summary of changes:
- Split Landlock into a two-phase flow:
  - `prepare_landlock(...)`: builds the Landlock ruleset and opens policy path FDs before privilege drop.
  - `apply_prepared_landlock(...)`: calls `restrict_self()` in the child process.
- Updated process spawn paths to:
  1. prepare Landlock in the supervisor/root context,
  2. enter network namespace in the child,
  3. apply prepared Landlock before dropping UID/GID,
  4. drop privileges,
  5. apply seccomp.
- Updated SSH PTY and non-PTY pre-exec paths to use the same order.
- Kept the existing one-shot `sandbox::apply(...)` path for compatibility.
- Added a visible `stderr` warning when Landlock fails in `best_effort`, while preserving the existing OCSF finding.
- Added temporary regression tests to validate prepare/build separation, ran them, then removed those temporary tests as requested.

Validation run:
- `cargo test -p openshell-sandbox sandbox::linux::landlock::tests -- --nocapture` passed after temporary tests were removed.
- `cargo test -p openshell-sandbox process::tests -- --nocapture` passed.
- `cargo check -p openshell-sandbox` passed.
- `cargo fmt -p openshell-sandbox` could not run because `cargo-fmt`/`rustfmt` is not installed for the active toolchain.

Notes:
- Existing unrelated warnings remain in the crate.
- There is an untracked `reports/` directory already present; I did not touch it.
- No commits were made.
